global_cues.us_markets: S&P 500 -0.41% to 7,108.40; Nasdaq -0.89% to 24,438.50; Dow -0.36% to 49,310.32. Pulled back from fresh intraday records as software stocks sold off and oil surged on renewed US-Iran war worries.
global_cues.european_markets: European bourses closed lower on Apr 23. Stoxx 600, FTSE, DAX and CAC all weighed down by Iran ceasefire uncertainty, higher oil and mixed earnings.
global_cues.asian_markets: GIFT Nifty trading at ~24,124 (-0.79%, -191 pts) in early Apr 24 session. Asian equities broadly retreating after reports of US tanker interceptions raised doubts over durability of Iran ceasefire; Nikkei/Hang Seng/CSI softening from prior session gains.

fii_dii_summary: Apr 23: FII net SELLERS at approx Rs -3,254.71 Cr (cash). DII net BUYERS at approx Rs +941.35 Cr. DII buying failed to fully absorb FII outflows; monthly FII outflow trend remains heavy (~Rs 1.17 lakh Cr pulled in March) amid West Asia crisis and weak rupee.
earnings_calendar: IndusInd Bank - 2026-04-24 (Q4 FY26, earnings call 5:00 PM IST)
Axis Bank - week of Apr 27-May 2 (Q4 FY26)
HCL Tech - week of Apr 27-May 2 (Q4 FY26)
UltraTech Cement - week of Apr 27-May 2 (Q4 FY26)
Prior day (Apr 23) results: Infosys, ICICI Bank (beat: PAT Rs 13,702 Cr), Tata Capital, Union Bank, Adani Energy, LTIM, IEX, Cyient - watch for stock-specific reactions
macro_events: RBI MPC (Apr 8) held repo rate at 5.25% for second consecutive meeting; stance neutral; flagged upside inflation risks from energy and weather
FY27 CPI forecast revised up to 4.6% vs 1.9% avg in 11MFY26
RBI barred banks/traders from offshore NDF participation on INR to curb speculative shorting of the rupee
GDP growth projections: Q1 6.8%, Q2 6.7%, Q3 7.0%, Q4 7.2%
No major domestic data prints scheduled for Apr 24; focus on Q4 earnings flow
sector_themes: Pharma STRONG: Nifty Pharma +2%+ on Apr 23 (Dr. Reddy's +8.87%, Cipla +5.72%) - defensive bid amid geopolitical risk
Banking/Financials WEAK: PSU Bank -2.19%, Private Bank & Financial Services -1%+; but ICICI Bank Q4 beat (PAT Rs 13,702 Cr) a positive standalone catalyst
Auto WEAK: Nifty Auto -2.35% on crude spike hurting margins/demand outlook
IT WEAK: Tech Mahindra among top Nifty losers; software sector selloff echoed from Wall Street
Insurance/Financial services theme: Jio Financial Services +4.19% on 50:50 JV with Allianz for general and health insurance
Energy/OMCs pressured by Brent >$100; upstream (ONGC, Oil India) beneficiaries
risk_flags: US-Iran conflict escalation: reports of US tanker interceptions threaten ceasefire; Strait of Hormuz closure risk remains live
Brent at $103.38/bbl (+1.44%); Indian crude basket at $103.05 (+2.63%) - India bought most expensive barrel in two decades in April
INR at worst level since FY12; RBI tightening offshore NDF channel to defend currency
Heavy FII outflows trend (Rs 1.17 lakh Cr in March) persisting into April
Nifty closed below 24,200 (at 24,173) breaking near-term support; GIFT Nifty pointing to further weak open near 24,120
Domestic stagflation risk: elevated oil feeding into inflation while RBI constrained from cutting rates
brief_summary: Indian markets set to open weak on Apr 24: GIFT Nifty ~-0.8%, tracking US selloff on renewed Iran war fears, Brent above $103, and FII outflows of ~Rs 3,254 Cr on Apr 23. Expect continued pressure on Banks, Auto and IT; Pharma remains the defensive outperformer and IndusInd Bank Q4 print is the key domestic event. Bias cautious/defensive with tight risk on long exposures until Hormuz/Iran situation clarifies.
